risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Randomization to MBSR or the health education control was performed using a random number generator. Personnel in the Penn State Hershey Medical Center Department of Public Health Sciences used Statistical Analysis System software version 9.2 proc plan to create a list based on permuted-blocks randomization scheme with subjects equally allocated to the two arms. Randomization to MBSR or health education control was stratified based on PCOS status (PCOS or non-PCOS)" Comment: This explicitly describes a proper random method (random number generator/software-generated permuted blocks with stratification), which is sufficient for Low risk for random sequence generation.

_evidence.method_quote: Randomization to MBSR or the health education control was performed using a random number generator. Personnel in the Penn State Hershey Medical Center Department of Public Health Sciences used Statistical Analysis System software version 9.2 proc plan to create a list based on permuted-blocks randomization scheme with subjects equally allocated to the two arms. Randomization to MBSR or health education control was stratified based on PCOS status (PCOS or non-PCOS) so that there would be an equivalent number of PCOS women in each arm of the study, which would allow the estimation of effect sizes of MBSR in PCOS. Personnel in the Department of Public Health Sciences selected a block size without revealing it to any of the blinded investigators or study personnel.
_evidence.method_summary: Random number generator with SAS proc plan, permuted-blocks randomization, and stratification by PCOS status.
context_clues: Statistical Analysis System software version 9.2 proc plan
equally allocated to the two arms

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Randomization to MBSR or health education control was performed by personnel in the Department of Public Health Sciences, and was not be communicated to research personnel who are collecting and reviewing outcomes data. Personnel in the Department of Public Health Sciences communicated the randomization scheme to the Class Scheduler who is not involved in the collection or review of outcomes data. The Class Scheduler communicated the study group assignments to the subjects and the instructors. The Class Scheduler, subjects and instructors were asked to keep the assignments concealed from the rest of the study personnel who are blinded." Comment: This describes an explicit mechanism in which assignment information was held by personnel not involved in outcome collection/review and communicated only through a class scheduler, which prevents foreknowledge by the recruiters/outcome personnel and is sufficient for low risk of allocation concealment.

_evidence.concealment_quote: Randomization to MBSR or the health education control was performed by personnel in the Department of Public Health Sciences, and was not be communicated to research personnel who are collecting and reviewing outcomes data. Personnel in the Department of Public Health Sciences communicated the randomization scheme to the Class Scheduler who is not involved in the collection or review of outcomes data. The Class Scheduler communicated the study group assignments to the subjects and the instructors. The Class Scheduler, subjects and instructors were asked to keep the assignments concealed from the rest of the study personnel who are blinded.
_evidence.note: The article describes sequence generation and an explicit communication process that kept assignments from blinded study personnel, but it does not describe envelopes or central allocation.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: “The Class Scheduler communicated the study group assignments to the subjects and the instructors.” and “The Class Scheduler, subjects and instructors were asked to keep the assignments concealed from the rest of the study personnel who are blinded.” Comment: Participants and instructors were informed of allocation, so they were not blinded. This could plausibly affect several reported outcomes that are subjective or patient-reported (e.g., Toronto Mindfulness Scale, SF-36, BSI-18, FFMQ, PROMIS sleep measures, FSDS, PSS-10, PANAS, perceived outcomes, and ratings/barriers), so the dominant study-level risk for performance bias is high despite some objective laboratory outcomes.

_evidence.blinding_quote: “The Class Scheduler communicated the study group assignments to the subjects and the instructors. The Class Scheduler, subjects and instructors were asked to keep the assignments concealed from the rest of the study personnel who are blinded. The principal investigator, study coordinator and all study personnel involved in the collection and review of outcomes data are blinded.”
_evidence.blinding_method: “The Class Scheduler communicated the study group assignments to the subjects and the instructors.” Control was “health education control” and MBSR was a separate intervention; no identical placebo/sham described.

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "The principal investigator, study coordinator and all study personnel involved in the collection and review of outcomes data are blinded." Comment: Outcome assessment was performed by blinded study personnel, and the study explicitly states that all personnel collecting/reviewing outcomes were blinded. The outcomes included both subjective patient-reported questionnaires (e.g. TMS, SF-36, BSI-18) and objective measures (e.g. HbA1c, fasting glucose, blood pressure), but because the outcome assessors were blinded, detection bias is unlikely across the assessed outcomes.

_evidence.assessor_blinding_quote: Randomization to MBSR or health education control was performed by personnel in the Department of Public Health Sciences, and was not be communicated to research personnel who are collecting and reviewing outcomes data. Personnel in the Department of Public Health Sciences communicated the randomization scheme to the Class Scheduler who is not involved in the collection or review of outcomes data. The Class Scheduler communicated the study group assignments to the subjects and the instructors. The Class Scheduler, subjects and instructors were asked to keep the assignments concealed from the rest of the study personnel who are blinded. The principal investigator, study coordinator and all study personnel involved in the collection and review of outcomes data are blinded.

risk_of_bias.support_text: Summary: The provided article text reports that 86 women were randomized (42 to MBSR, 44 to health education control) and that the analysis would follow an intention-to-treat principle with linear mixed-effects models, but it does not report per-arm analysed numbers at the outcome time point, numbers lost to follow-up, or reasons for missing outcome data for the analysed outcomes. Comment: Because actual post-randomization attrition on the analysed outcomes cannot be quantified from the text, the risk of attrition bias cannot be judged as Low or High and is therefore Unclear.

_evidence.notes: No CONSORT-style attrition flow or per-arm analyzed/lost numbers were reported in the provided article text. The Results state only that 86 women were randomized and that the trial was ongoing; the Methods specify intent-to-treat analysis.
